evals.prompt: Build me a recipe sharing website. Users should be able to post recipes with ingredients and steps, browse recipes by category, and save their favorites. Use React and Node.js.
evals.expected_output: A complete full-stack recipe sharing app with: (1) React frontend with distinct visual design, recipe cards, category filters, and favorite/save UI; (2) Node.js/Express backend with REST API endpoints for CRUD on recipes, user auth, and favorites; (3) PostgreSQL schema with users, recipes, ingredients, categories, and favorites tables; (4) JWT authentication; (5) .env.example and README with setup instructions.
assertions: The response includes a stack choice with brief rationale
A visual/aesthetic direction is stated before code (e.g. 'editorial', 'warm', 'minimal')
Frontend code includes at least one CSS custom property color system (--color-bg, --color-accent, etc.)
Font choice is specified and avoids Inter, Roboto, Arial, or system-ui as display font
API endpoints are listed or documented (GET /api/recipes, POST /api/recipes, etc.)
A database schema is provided for recipes, users, and favorites
Auth endpoints are included (register, login, refresh or similar)
Input validation is present on at least one API handler
A .env.example file or equivalent environment variable list is provided
Setup/run instructions are included

evals.prompt: I need a SaaS landing page for a project management tool called 'Orbit'. It should have a hero section, features grid, pricing table (3 tiers), testimonials, and a waitlist signup form. Make it look premium and modern. No backend needed, just store signups in Supabase.
evals.expected_output: A polished single-page landing site with: (1) clear aesthetic direction (not generic purple gradients); (2) hero, features, pricing (3 tiers with toggle monthly/annual), testimonials, and waitlist form sections; (3) Supabase integration for form submission; (4) fully responsive CSS; (5) distinctive typography pairing; (6) animations or micro-interactions; (7) complete HTML/CSS/JS or React code.
assertions: All five sections are implemented: hero, features, pricing, testimonials, waitlist/signup
Pricing section has at least 3 tiers with feature lists
An aesthetic direction is named or clearly evident (not generic purple gradient on white)
Two distinct fonts are used (display and body)
The form submission connects to Supabase or equivalent (code shown, not just described)
Layout is responsive (uses media queries or CSS grid/flexbox with mobile consideration)
At least one animation or transition effect is included
The response does not use Inter or Roboto as the primary display typeface

evals.prompt: Create a full-stack expense tracker app. Users log in, add expenses with categories and amounts, and see a monthly breakdown chart. I want to use Next.js with a PostgreSQL database.
evals.expected_output: A full Next.js App Router application with: (1) auth (login/register pages); (2) dashboard with expense list and a chart (e.g. Chart.js or Recharts); (3) add/edit expense form with category selection; (4) Prisma schema with users, expenses, and categories; (5) Server Actions or API routes for data operations; (6) monthly aggregation query; (7) .env.example and setup instructions.
assertions: Stack is Next.js with App Router (not Pages Router)
Prisma schema is provided with users and expenses tables
Auth is implemented (login and register pages/routes)
A dashboard page shows expense list
A chart or visualization for monthly breakdown is included (library specified)
Server Actions or API route handlers are shown for creating expenses
Monthly aggregation logic is present (groupBy month or equivalent)
Password hashing is used (bcrypt or argon2, never plaintext)
.env.example includes DATABASE_URL and auth secret
The UI has a stated aesthetic direction distinct from generic dashboard styles
